revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Princess Nakashi]][[fa:شاهدخت ناکاشی]][[ja:中磯皇女]][[zh:中矶皇女]] {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|中磯皇女|date=January 2023}}
{{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Nakashi |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] |reign = 455–456 |spouse = [[:en:Emperor Ankō|Emperor Ankō]] |issue = |royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[:en:Emperor Richū|Emperor Richū]] |mother = [[:en:Princess Kusakanohatabino|Princess Kusakanohatabino]] |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = after 455 |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}}
'''Princess Nakashi''' (? – after 455) was [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Ankō|Emperor Ankō]].<ref>黛弘道 (1968-12). “継体天皇の系譜について : 釈日本紀所引上宮記逸文の研究”. 学習院史学 (学習院大学史学会) 5: 1-14. ISSN 0286-1658. NAID 110007562716.</ref> Nakashi was reviously married to Prince Ōkusaka, son of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. According to the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihongi]], Emperor Ankō had Okusaka executed (believing false accusations of disobedience), and, possibly in regret for unjustly executing Okusaka, took Nakashi as his own.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=roEfAAAAYAAJ&dq=Nakashi+anko&pg=PA331 |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Williams |first=Yoko |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QVtUAQAAQBAJ&dq=Nakashi+anko&pg=PA88 |title=Tsumi - Offence and Retribution in Early Japan |date=2013-10-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-136-87422-2 |language=en}}</ref> She became a concubine of Emperor Ankō in 454 and was appointed empress 455. Emperor Anko came to love her greatly.<ref name=":0" />
Nakashi already had a child with her first husband, Okusaka. This child, Prince Mayuwa, was thereafter raised in the imperial palace.<ref name=":0" />
She was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Richū|Emperor Richū]] and [[:en:Princess Kusakanohatabino|Princess Kusakanohatabino]].
==Notes== {{reflist}}
{{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Kusaka no Hatabi no hime]] | years=455–456}} {{s-end}}
{{Consorts of Japan}}{{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Daughters of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:5th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:5th-century Japanese people]]
[[Category:Completion tier 1]]
}}

[[en:Princess Nakashi]]

{{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Nakashi |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] |reign = 455–456 |spouse = [[:en:Emperor Ankō|Emperor Ankō]] |issue = |royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[:en:Emperor Richū|Emperor Richū]] |mother = [[:en:Princess Kusakanohatabino|Princess Kusakanohatabino]] |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = after 455 |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}}
'''Princess Nakashi''' (? – after 455) was [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Ankō|Emperor Ankō]].<ref>黛弘道 (1968-12). “継体天皇の系譜について : 釈日本紀所引上宮記逸文の研究”. 学習院史学 (学習院大学史学会) 5: 1-14. ISSN 0286-1658. NAID 110007562716.</ref> Nakashi was reviously married to Prince Ōkusaka, son of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. According to the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihongi]], Emperor Ankō had Okusaka executed (believing false accusations of disobedience), and, possibly in regret for unjustly executing Okusaka, took Nakashi as his own.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=roEfAAAAYAAJ&dq=Nakashi+anko&pg=PA331 |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Williams |first=Yoko |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QVtUAQAAQBAJ&dq=Nakashi+anko&pg=PA88 |title=Tsumi - Offence and Retribution in Early Japan |date=2013-10-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-136-87422-2 |language=en}}</ref> She became a concubine of Emperor Ankō in 454 and was appointed empress 455. Emperor Anko came to love her greatly.<ref name=":0" />
Nakashi already had a child with her first husband, Okusaka. This child, Prince Mayuwa, was thereafter raised in the imperial palace.<ref name=":0" />
She was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Richū|Emperor Richū]] and [[:en:Princess Kusakanohatabino|Princess Kusakanohatabino]].
==Notes== {{reflist}}

[[en:Princess Nakashi]]
{{wikidata link|Q11366830}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Princess Nakashi]][[fa:شاهدخت ناکاشی]][[ja:中磯皇女]][[zh:中矶皇女]] {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|中磯皇女|date=January 2023}}
{{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Nakashi |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] |reign = 455–456 |spouse = [[:en:Emperor Ankō|Emperor Ankō]] |issue = |royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[:en:Emperor Richū|Emperor Richū]] |mother = [[:en:Princess Kusakanohatabino|Princess Kusakanohatabino]] |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = after 455 |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}}
'''Princess Nakashi''' (? – after 455) was [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Ankō|Emperor Ankō]].<ref>黛弘道 (1968-12). “継体天皇の系譜について : 釈日本紀所引上宮記逸文の研究”. 学習院史学 (学習院大学史学会) 5: 1-14. ISSN 0286-1658. NAID 110007562716.</ref> Nakashi was reviously married to Prince Ōkusaka, son of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. According to the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihongi]], Emperor Ankō had Okusaka executed (believing false accusations of disobedience), and, possibly in regret for unjustly executing Okusaka, took Nakashi as his own.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=roEfAAAAYAAJ&dq=Nakashi+anko&pg=PA331 |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Williams |first=Yoko |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QVtUAQAAQBAJ&dq=Nakashi+anko&pg=PA88 |title=Tsumi - Offence and Retribution in Early Japan |date=2013-10-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-136-87422-2 |language=en}}</ref> She became a concubine of Emperor Ankō in 454 and was appointed empress 455. Emperor Anko came to love her greatly.<ref name=":0" />
Nakashi already had a child with her first husband, Okusaka. This child, Prince Mayuwa, was thereafter raised in the imperial palace.<ref name=":0" />
She was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Richū|Emperor Richū]] and [[:en:Princess Kusakanohatabino|Princess Kusakanohatabino]].
==Notes== {{reflist}}
{{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Kusaka no Hatabi no hime]] | years=455–456}} {{s-end}}

{{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Nakashi |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] |reign = 455–456 |spouse = [[:en:Emperor Ankō|Emperor Ankō]] |issue = |royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[:en:Emperor Richū|Emperor Richū]] |mother = [[:en:Princess Kusakanohatabino|Princess Kusakanohatabino]] |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = after 455 |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}}
'''Princess Nakashi''' (? – after 455) was [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Ankō|Emperor Ankō]].<ref>黛弘道 (1968-12). “継体天皇の系譜について : 釈日本紀所引上宮記逸文の研究”. 学習院史学 (学習院大学史学会) 5: 1-14. ISSN 0286-1658. NAID 110007562716.</ref> Nakashi was reviously married to Prince Ōkusaka, son of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. According to the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihongi]], Emperor Ankō had Okusaka executed (believing false accusations of disobedience), and, possibly in regret for unjustly executing Okusaka, took Nakashi as his own.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=roEfAAAAYAAJ&dq=Nakashi+anko&pg=PA331 |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Williams |first=Yoko |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QVtUAQAAQBAJ&dq=Nakashi+anko&pg=PA88 |title=Tsumi - Offence and Retribution in Early Japan |date=2013-10-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-136-87422-2 |language=en}}</ref> She became a concubine of Emperor Ankō in 454 and was appointed empress 455. Emperor Anko came to love her greatly.<ref name=":0" />
Nakashi already had a child with her first husband, Okusaka. This child, Prince Mayuwa, was thereafter raised in the imperial palace.<ref name=":0" />
She was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Richū|Emperor Richū]] and [[:en:Princess Kusakanohatabino|Princess Kusakanohatabino]].
==Notes== {{reflist}}

{{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|中磯皇女|date=January 2023}}

{{Infobox royalty
|consort         = yes
|name            = Nakashi

|reign           = 455–456
|spouse          = {{ill|Emperor Ankō|en|Emperor Ankō|WD=Q361010|lt=Emperor Ankō}}
|issue           =
|royal house     = {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan}}
|father          = {{ill|Emperor Richū|en|Emperor Richū|WD=Q329704|lt=Emperor Richū}}
|mother          = {{ill|Princess Kusakanohatabino|en|Princess Kusakanohatabino|WD=Q11618292|lt=Princess Kusakanohatabino}}
|birth_date      = 
|birth_place     = 
|death_date      = after 455
|death_place     =
|date of burial  =
|place of burial =
|}}

'''Princess Nakashi''' (? – after 455) was {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan}} as the consort of {{ill|Emperor Ankō|en|Emperor Ankō|WD=Q361010|lt=Emperor Ankō}}.<ref>黛弘道 (1968-12). “継体天皇の系譜について : 釈日本紀所引上宮記逸文の研究”. 学習院史学 (学習院大学史学会) 5: 1-14. ISSN 0286-1658. NAID 110007562716.</ref> Nakashi was reviously married to Prince Ōkusaka, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}.  According to the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihongi]], Emperor Ankō had Okusaka executed (believing false accusations of disobedience), and, possibly in regret for unjustly executing Okusaka, took Nakashi as his own.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=roEfAAAAYAAJ&dq=Nakashi+anko&pg=PA331 |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Williams |first=Yoko |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QVtUAQAAQBAJ&dq=Nakashi+anko&pg=PA88 |title=Tsumi - Offence and Retribution in Early Japan |date=2013-10-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-136-87422-2 |language=en}}</ref> She became a concubine of Emperor Ankō in 454 and was appointed empress 455.  Emperor Anko came to love her greatly.<ref name=":0" /> 

Nakashi already had a child with her first husband, Okusaka.  This child, Prince Mayuwa, was thereafter raised in the imperial palace.<ref name=":0" />

She was the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Richū|en|Emperor Richū|WD=Q329704|lt=Emperor Richū}} and {{ill|Princess Kusakanohatabino|en|Princess Kusakanohatabino|WD=Q11618292|lt=Princess Kusakanohatabino}}.

==Notes==
{{reflist}}

{{s-start}}
{{s-roy|jp}}
{{succession box 
| before= [[Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]] 
| title={{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress consort of Japan}} 
| after=[[Kusaka no Hatabi no hime]] 
| years=455–456}}
{{s-end}}

'''Princess Nakashi''' (? – after 455) was {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan|1=Empress of Japan}} as the consort of {{ill|Emperor Ankō|en|Emperor Ankō|WD=Q361010|lt=Emperor Ankō|1=Emperor Ankō}}.<ref>黛弘道 (1968-12). “継体天皇の系譜について : 釈日本紀所引上宮記逸文の研究”. 学習院史学 (学習院大学史学会) 5: 1-14. ISSN 0286-1658. NAID 110007562716.</ref> Nakashi was reviously married to Prince Ōkusaka, son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}.  According to the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihongi]], Emperor Ankō had Okusaka executed (believing false accusations of disobedience), and, possibly in regret for unjustly executing Okusaka, took Nakashi as his own.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=roEfAAAAYAAJ&dq=Nakashi+anko&pg=PA331 |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Williams |first=Yoko |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QVtUAQAAQBAJ&dq=Nakashi+anko&pg=PA88 |title=Tsumi - Offence and Retribution in Early Japan |date=2013-10-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-136-87422-2 |language=en}}</ref> She became a concubine of Emperor Ankō in 454 and was appointed empress 455.  Emperor Anko came to love her greatly.<ref name=":0" /> 

Nakashi already had a child with her first husband, Okusaka.  This child, Prince Mayuwa, was thereafter raised in the imperial palace.<ref name=":0" />

She was the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Richū|en|Emperor Richū|WD=Q329704|lt=Emperor Richū|1=Emperor Richū}} and {{ill|Princess Kusakanohatabino|en|Princess Kusakanohatabino|WD=Q11618292|lt=Princess Kusakanohatabino|1=Princess Kusakanohatabino}}.
